vBRIEFInfo.author: panopticon-cli/0.0.0
vBRIEFInfo.description: Plan for PAN-513: Memory monitoring: show available RAM in dashboard + guard rails before spawning agents

plan.title: Memory monitoring: show available RAM in dashboard + guard rails before spawning agents
plan.author: agent:claude-opus-4-6

narratives.Problem: Spawning multiple planning agents simultaneously can exhaust 64GB system RAM, causing systemd memory pressure and full system crash. No memory visibility in the main dashboard and no pre-spawn guardrails exist.
narratives.Proposal: Add a memory indicator pill in the dashboard header, a two-tier pre-spawn memory guard (warning at 4GB free, hard block at 2GB free), a sticky warning banner with top memory consumers, and configurable thresholds via ~/.panopticon.env.

items.title: Extend system health endpoint with memFree and threshold config

narrative.Action: Add memFree (bytes) to the godview system-health cache response in misc.ts. Add getMemoryThresholds() to env-loader.ts that reads PAN_MEMORY_WARN_GB (default 4) and PAN_MEMORY_BLOCK_GB (default 2) from ~/.panopticon.env. Include thresholds in the health response so the frontend can evaluate warning/critical states.

subItems.title: Health response includes warnThresholdBytes and blockThresholdBytes from env config
Thresholds default to 4GB warn / 2GB block when env vars not set

items.title: Add two-tier memory guard to POST /api/agents

narrative.Action: In routes/agents.ts POST /api/agents handler, after existing validation guards and before workspace creation: check os.freemem() against thresholds. If below critical (2GB default), return 422 with memoryBlocked: true, current stats, and Fly.io hint. If below warning (4GB default) and request does not include bypassMemoryWarning: true, return 200 with memoryWarning: true and stats (frontend shows confirmation). If bypassMemoryWarning: true is set, proceed normally.

subItems.title: Spawning agent when RAM < critical threshold returns 422 with memoryBlocked flag
Spawning agent when RAM < warning threshold returns memoryWarning requiring confirmation
Passing bypassMemoryWarning: true skips warning tier and proceeds with spawn
Critical block response includes Fly.io remote offloading hint message

items.title: Create MemoryIndicator pill component in dashboard header

narrative.Action: Create MemoryIndicator.tsx — a compact pill showing 'XX / YY GB' with a color-coded dot (green > 30% free, yellow 15-30%, red < 15%). Polls GET /api/godview/system-health every 10s via React Query or a shared hook. Add it to Header.tsx between CloisterStatusBar and FreshnessIndicator. Tooltip shows exact percentages.

subItems.title: Dashboard header shows live RAM usage (used / total) with color-coded status dot
Color transitions: green > 30% free, yellow 15-30%, red < 15%

narrative.Action: Create MemoryWarningBanner.tsx — a sticky amber/red banner rendered in App.tsx below the Header when system memory is below the warning threshold. Shows 'System memory low: X.X GB free of Y GB'. Lists top 3-5 running agents as memory consumers with Kill action buttons (calls existing agent stop endpoint). Dismissible, but re-appears if memory drops further. Uses the same health polling data as MemoryIndicator.

subItems.title: Warning banner appears when RAM is below warning threshold
Banner lists top memory consumers with kill action buttons
Banner is dismissible but re-appears if memory drops further

items.title: Handle memory warning response in spawn UI with confirmation dialog

narrative.Action: In the frontend spawn flow (KanbanBoard, IssueAgentCard, or wherever POST /api/agents is called): when response contains memoryWarning: true, show a confirmation dialog via DialogProvider explaining current memory state and asking to proceed or cancel. On confirm, re-submit with bypassMemoryWarning: true. When response contains memoryBlocked: true (422), show an error toast with the block message and Fly.io hint. No retry option for blocked state.

subItems.title: Warning-tier spawn shows confirmation dialog with memory stats before proceeding
Critical-tier block shows error message with Fly.io remote hint

narrative.Action: Add PAN_MEMORY_WARN_GB and PAN_MEMORY_BLOCK_GB to the example env file or settings documentation so users know the thresholds are configurable.
